verse_specific_anchors: śiṣyas te 'haṁ — quoted verbatim in the engineering scenario as the request-formulation that unlocks teaching
prapanna / surrendered — the operator must explicitly downgrade their own positional rank
kārpaṇya-doṣa — 'systematically beaten down by my own smallness' renders the kārpaṇya as the prerequisite affective state
saṁmūḍha-cetāḥ — 'judgment regarding the correct course of action has evaporated entirely' renders the cognitive bewilderment
śādhi mām — 'instruct me' / 'teach me' as imperative, not advice-request
verdict_reason: I tested this against three other verses I would expect a lazy author to attach this analog to: BG 4.34 (tad viddhi praṇipātena — 'know that by humble approach'), BG 18.66 (sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ekam śaraṇam vraja — 'abandon all dharmas, take refuge in Me alone'), and BG 9.34 (man-manā bhava — 'be devoted to me'). All three involve surrender or approach-to-teacher, but each has distinct anchors: 4.34 anchors on the *threefold method* of approach (paripraśnena, sevayā, praṇipātena); 18.66 anchors on the abandonment of all prior dharmas; 9.34 anchors on devotional absorption. This engineering analog explicitly names the request-formulation as the unlock, anchors on śiṣyas te 'haṁ as the operative phrase, and structures around the kārpaṇya-saṁmūḍha-prapanna sequence — that is verse-2.7-specific, not transferable. The 'four weeks blocked on database sharding' scenario instantiates the saṁmūḍha-cetāḥ + kārpaṇya combination, not generic surrender. PASS.
